title: Implement Incremental Load Logic
description: Activate incremental load capability using existing pipeline parameters (p_full_load, p_from_date) to eliminate full refresh overhead. Design phase complete with comprehensive strategy.
acceptanceCriteria: Incremental load working for procurement data (bronze, silver, gold layers)
High-water mark tracking implemented in bronze_load_metadata table
No data duplication after multiple incremental loads
Performance improvement >50% for incremental vs full load (expected: 94% time reduction)
Pipeline parameter wiring complete (p_full_load controls load behavior)
Documentation complete with strategy and logic in incremental_load_strategy.md
dependencies: Azure SQL source has Date field for filtering
Delta Lake format supports MERGE operations (already using Delta)
No schema changes during incremental loads
notes: Design phase ✅ COMPLETE - 17 tables analyzed, merge strategies defined
Expected time savings: 94% (9 min → 30 sec) for incremental loads
Focus on procurement data only - highest value for incremental load
External datasets (EPI, WGI, EU) remain full refresh (annual updates)
CLAUDE: Write dataflow modifications, notebook MERGE logic, pipeline parameter wiring
ERIK: Deploy to Fabric, run test loads (full + incremental), verify no duplicates

subtasks.title: Modify bronze dataflow with date parameter filtering
subtasks.details: Update bronze_azureSQLdb2table.Dataflow to use @p_from_date parameter with SQL pushdown

subtasks.title: Implement silver layer merge logic
subtasks.details: Update clean_columnsAndHeaders.Notebook with Delta MERGE operations and high-water mark tracking

subtasks.title: Implement gold layer incremental updates
subtasks.details: Update silver-to-gold2.Notebook to handle incremental fact/dimension updates with merge logic

subtasks.title: Wire pipeline parameters to all activities
subtasks.details: Connect p_full_load and p_from_date parameters, implement conditional logic

subtasks.title: Test and validate performance gains
subtasks.details: Run full vs incremental load tests, verify no duplicates, measure time savings
